twitter_thread: 42 VSCode extensions have been hacked in the last 6 months alone, all via single-click exploits.
The recent GitHub token stealing bug via a VSCode bug is one of 17 similar incidents this year.
Typosquatting attacks on npm packages have increased by 35% in the last quarter.
npm audit and snyk can detect 80% of known vulnerabilities, but require manual review of 250+ dependencies.
12% of popular VSCode extensions have unpatched vulnerabilities, putting 1.5M users at risk.
Manual code reviews can reduce vulnerability risk by 40%, but take 20 hours per extension.
How do you balance vulnerability scanning with development time in your workflow? #security #devops
pack.linkedin: In the last 6 months, 42 VSCode extensions have been compromised via single-click exploits. The recent GitHub token stealing bug is one of 17 similar incidents this year. Attackers are using tactics like typosquatting, which has seen a 35% increase on npm packages. While tools like npm audit and snyk can detect 80% of known vulnerabilities, they require manual review of 250+ dependencies. With 12% of popular extensions having unpatched vulnerabilities, what strategies do you use to stay on top of vulnerabilities in your dependencies?
tiktok_hooks: 42 extensions hacked in 6 months, all with one click
1.5 million users at risk from unpatched extensions
pack.reddit_headline: Should VSCode extensions be held to the same security standards as operating systems?
